occurrence_sentence: When Sam Brannan announced to the world: _'Gold! Gold! Gold from the American River!'_ , half the world's population (or so it seemed to the tiny California population which lived there at the time) descended on the soon to be the newest state of the union.

occurrence_sentence: Dollar investment in the QC space from 2012 to last year, again referring to the [Nature](https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-019-02935-4) analysis harvested from data available at the[ Quantum Computing Report](https://quantumcomputingreport.com/), the [Boston Consulting Group](https://www.bcg.com/), [PitchBook](https://get.pitchbook.com/pitchbook-data/?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=EG-Brand-UK&utm_term=pitchbook&_bt=310114431597&_bm=e&_bn=g&gclid=Cj0KCQjwr-_tBRCMARIsAN413WQ_fh_KatEdPpnEOcM_WV2SrETY5nsXoEbCfjshM5w3ixAVAjSIcSoaAmuSEALw_wcB) and [Crunchbase](https://www.crunchbase.com/), shows that the share of the cash invested in North America totalled more than $330 million, with Europe, Australia and China next in the ranking, respectively.

interaction_phrase: owner of the Quantum Computing Report
occurrence_sentence: 'There is a lot of hype right now. Quantum technologies have seen rapid progress, but machines that can tackle many kinds of computation are still likely to be decades away, and even then it will be difficult to write algorithms that harness their abilities… There are now hints that US firms are finding it harder to get private funding.' — Doug Finke, a computer scientist and owner of the Quantum Computing Report

interaction_phrase: bigger players like Google, IBM, Rigetti and D-Wave Systems
occurrence_sentence: One of the biggest dangers is to the small and medium-sized hardware-agnostic startups whose business is based on designing and developing quantum software to support and service the hardware applications built by the bigger players like Google, IBM, Rigetti and D-Wave Systems.

1QBit, a Canadian QC startup based in Vancouver, is one such use case. Founded by Andrew Fursman and Landon Downs in 2012, 1QBit creates QC algorithms software for companies that see a future in utilizing quantum hardware architectural systems.
